schema_notice: Phase B0 method-novelty threat matrix. One record per paper. Controlled vocabularies: evidence_quality in {code_inspected, full_text_inspected_via_fetch, abstract_reviewed, search_summary_only, prior_a3_matrix_reused}; overlap fields in {HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, NONE, UNKNOWN} optionally followed by ' -- <qualifier>'. Boolean fields are true/false/null (null = insufficient evidence). Every YES/NO/PARTIAL-style conclusion cites its evidence in the 'evidence' array or is a checked-absence statement naming what was checked. WebFetch results were produced by a fetch-and-summarize tool that returns verbatim quotes; this is recorded as an evidence limitation in docs/B0_SEARCH_LOG.md. This file is a Phase B0 artifact and does not modify docs/related_work_matrix.json (frozen A3 artifact).

candidates.M1: Residual causal-utility protection: predict the residual between offline counterfactual KV-ablation utility and an existing deployable compression score; spend a strictly budgeted rescue partition on predicted false negatives at matched physical KV bytes.
candidates.M2: Interaction-aware dynamic rescue: recompute causal-residual risk for borderline states conditional on the current compressed cache after each compaction, inside a fixed byte budget.
candidates.M3: Faithfulness-constrained memory allocation: allocate token/layer/head memory under a fixed physical byte budget subject to bounded degradation of a preregistered causal reasoning-dependence proxy.

papers.title: ArborKV: Structure-Aware KV Cache Management for Scaling Tree-based LLM Reasoning
papers.code_source: UNKNOWN -- no repository URL found in abstract or HTML full text
papers.offline_supervision: Leave-one-out masking at thought-block level on full-retention trajectories: 'the KV cache corresponding to each closed thought block i is individually zeroed out, and the resulting change in answer accuracy is measured' -- calibrates linear MSVE weights theta.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH -- performs offline counterfactual KV-block ablation (answer-accuracy delta) as supervision for a lightweight learned estimator used online; this is M1's supervision pipeline minus the residual framing
papers.m2_overlap: MEDIUM -- policy update events (boundary/transition/pressure) reallocate dynamically, but the learned score itself is not conditioned on prior evictions ('after calibration, theta is fixed during inference')
papers.m3_overlap: MEDIUM -- tree-aware allocation under a fixed budget, but no faithfulness/causal-dependence constraint
papers.strongest_overlap: M1: 'use offline KV ablation to supervise an online importance predictor' is already implemented (MSVE).
papers.substantive_distinction: Intrinsically tree-specific (distance-to-active-leaf term e^(-lambda*Delta_i) is meaningless in linear CoT); predicts absolute block utility, not residual over an existing compressor's score; no false-negative-targeted rescue partition; lazy rehydration presupposes revisit events that linear decoding lacks.
claims_killed: M1 sub-claim: novelty of offline leave-one-out KV ablation as supervision for a deployable lightweight importance estimator
M1 sub-claim: novelty of KV-ablation labels measured by answer-behavior change
claims_surviving: M1 residual-over-baseline formulation (checked: ArborKV full text describes absolute MSVE score si = clip(sigma(theta^T phi_i),0,1), no baseline-compressor residualization)
M2 eviction-conditional importance (checked: features are block content + tree topology only)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2605.22106v1 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes on MSVE calibration ('We calibrate theta offline using hindsight interventions'), leave-one-out KV zeroing, fixed theta at inference, tree-specificity, lazy rehydration.
uncertainties: Fetch-summarizer mediated; full appendix (Sec 5.1 calibration protocol details, trajectory counts) not independently read
No code available to confirm implementation details

papers.title: IntentKV: Cross-Turn Intent-Aware KV Cache Pruning for Agent Inference
papers.code_source: UNKNOWN -- paper mentions a released artifact; no URL captured
papers.offline_supervision: Future-action labels: history tokens matching literal substrings of the next five tool-call arguments in ToolBench traces; BCE + ranking loss on the residual head only, base LLM frozen.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH -- implements exactly the residual-over-baseline architecture: s_j = rule_j + alpha*MLP(phi_j (+) c_j), zero-initialized so 'the pruner exactly recovers [the rule] on day zero', explicitly trained to 'learn a correction for cases the rule scorer misses'
papers.m2_overlap: LOW -- scores recomputed per prefill event across turns, but not conditioned on prior eviction decisions within a decode
papers.strongest_overlap: M1: the residual-correction-of-a-deployable-heuristic operation, including zero-init recovery of the base score and explicit false-negative framing, already exists as a published cache method.
papers.substantive_distinction: Supervision is observational future-use substring matching, not counterfactual KV ablation; domain is multi-turn agent tool-use histories, not single-trace reasoning decode; correction re-ranks globally rather than through a strictly budgeted rescue partition.
claims_killed: M1 sub-claim: novelty of predicting a residual correction to an existing deployable retention score to fix cases the base score systematically misses
claims_surviving: M1 sub-claim: counterfactual (interventional) labels for the residual (checked: IntentKV labels are substring-match future-use, no ablation)
M1 sub-claim: strictly budgeted rescue partition (checked: 'No dedicated rescue partition ... all tokens compete in a global top-k selection')
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2606.09916v1 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for score equation, zero-initialization, supervision labels, forced-set selection formula, slot-map eviction.
uncertainties: Fetch-summarizer mediated; ablation table read only in summary form

papers.title: ForesightKV: Optimizing KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models by Learning Long-Term Contribution
papers.code_source: https://github.com/RUCAIBox/ForesightKV (URL from paper; repository not inspected)
intervention_type: post_eviction_loss_increase_reward (counterfactual effect of eviction on future token loss)
papers.offline_supervision: Two stages: (1) pairwise-ranking distillation of Golden Eviction future-attention scores into an MLP; (2) GRPO RL where reward penalizes squared loss increase on low-entropy tokens 'whose loss increase from original to evicted model exceeds threshold eta'.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH -- learned future-utility eviction for reasoning models (including DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B) with a counterfactual eviction-effect quantity (post-eviction loss increase) inside the training reward
papers.m2_overlap: HIGH -- the GRPO stage is an MDP whose state is 'the current remaining KV cache at step t': eviction decisions are explicitly conditional on the compressed cache, sequential compaction after compaction
papers.m3_overlap: LOW -- fixed budget enforced, no allocation-optimization framing
papers.strongest_overlap: M2's central operation (importance conditional on the current compressed cache, refreshed across sequential evictions, for reasoning models) is already implemented and published; also removes M1's 'no one uses counterfactual eviction-effect supervision' argument.
papers.substantive_distinction: Predicts absolute importance, not residual over a fixed base compressor ('scores are used directly for top-K multinomial sampling'); oracle labels are future attention, not controlled KV ablation of answer behavior; no false-negative rescue partition beyond recency protection.
claims_killed: M2 central claim: novelty of eviction decisions conditional on the current compressed cache state, sequentially refreshed, for reasoning models
M1 sub-claim: novelty of using a counterfactual post-eviction behavioral-degradation quantity in the training signal of a deployable eviction policy
claims_surviving: M1 residual-over-baseline formulation (checked: absolute scoring, no base-compressor residualization)
M1 controlled per-state KV-ablation labels (ForesightKV's reward aggregates over evicted sets chosen by the policy, not per-state leave-one-out labels)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2602.03203v2 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for Golden Eviction (Eq. 5), MDP state definition, GRPO reward, online MLP inputs (Eq. 1), evaluation on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B / Qwen3 at 1K-4K budgets.
uncertainties: Fetch-summarizer mediated; official repository not inspected

papers.title: ThinKV: Thought-Adaptive KV Cache Compression for Efficient Reasoning Models
papers.version_reviewed: v2 (2026-05-07); latest version reviewed per task requirement, not only the v1 abstract
papers.code_source: UNKNOWN -- no repository link found in v2 full text
intervention_type: offline_counterfactual_segment_ablation: 'KL divergence between A's distributions obtained with and without Y_i, averaged over 50 rollouts'
papers.offline_supervision: Offline calibration on ~100 prompts: KDE sparsity thresholds for thought classification + counterfactual KL importance hierarchy per thought TYPE (static ranks rho(R)=2, rho(E)=1, rho(T)=0); no trained predictor.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH -- counterfactual KV-relevant segment ablation with answer-distribution comparison already directly determines a deployed compression policy for reasoning models; also observes instance-level exceptions (high-importance backtracking T-thoughts whose removal 'causes the model to loop endlessly')
papers.m2_overlap: MEDIUM -- categories refreshed every tau=128 steps, but 'No re-scoring after eviction... scores are not conditional on compressed cache state'
papers.m3_overlap: MEDIUM -- precision/retention allocation by thought type under a budget, but no causal-dependence constraint in the allocation objective
papers.strongest_overlap: M1: 'measure counterfactual importance of reasoning segments by comparing answer distributions with and without the segment, and use it to set the deployed policy' is published; M1 cannot claim the counterfactual-supervision-to-policy pipeline.
papers.substantive_distinction: Importance is static per thought category at deployment (instance-specific outliers handled by a uniform minimum-retention floor, not re-scored); no learned per-instance predictor; no residualization against a base compressor; no false-negative rescue partition.
claims_killed: M1 sub-claim: novelty of counterfactual KV-segment ablation with answer-distribution comparison as the source of a deployed compression policy on reasoning models
Generic claim: 'dynamically classify thought types' (already the paper's core)
claims_surviving: M1 instance-specific residual prediction (checked: ThinKV deploys static per-category ranks; per-instance causal labels are not predicted online)
M2 post-eviction conditional rescoring (checked: refresh is interval-based, not eviction-conditional)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2510.01290v2 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for KL counterfactual measurement, static rank mapping, tau=128 refresh, eviction cases 1/2, no-training statement, memory accounting.
uncertainties: Fetch-summarizer mediated; direct PDF read failed (file too large for the fetch tool), HTML v2 used instead

papers.title: CASK: Core-Aware Selective KV Compression for Reasoning Traces
importance_signal: core_vs_scratch_partition (selection signal UNSPECIFIED in abstract and README)
intervention_type: none_for_policy (evaluation harness does teacher-forced fixed-trace replay -- see A3 matrix)
papers.offline_supervision: None found -- 'No information provided in the README about learned parameters or training procedures'; counterfactual supervision for core selection not mentioned anywhere inspected.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- the protected core is already a budgeted protected partition of reasoning states framed as anchoring answer formation; M1's rescue partition would be reviewed as a renamed protected core with a different selection rule
papers.m2_overlap: UNKNOWN -- refresh behavior not documented in inspected sources
papers.strongest_overlap: M1's budgeted protected partition for reasoning-critical KV states; also owns the fixed-trace replay diagnostic (A3, already killed this repo's diagnostic claim).
papers.substantive_distinction: Core selection is not (as far as inspected) supervised by counterfactual ablation, and CASK consolidates scratch rather than rescuing base-policy false negatives; its safety diagnostics (lost representative mass, kappa-dispersion) are geometric, not causal-utility, quantities.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'preserve a protected reasoning core' (already CASK's headline operation)
claims_surviving: M1 causal-ablation-supervised selection of the protected set (checked absence: abstract, README, and A3 code inspection of the evaluation harness show no ablation supervision; UNCERTAIN pending full paper text)
evidence: A3 direct code inspection of scripts/replay_reference_fidelity.py (docs/RELATED_WORK_MATRIX.md par. 6.1/8)
WebFetch of arxiv abstract and github.com/Skyline-23/CASK README (2026-07-19): core/scratch quotes, two-stage pipeline, diagnostics 'lost representative mass' and 'kappa-dispersion'.
uncertainties: Core-selection signal and refresh schedule unspecified in all inspected sources; full paper method section not read (no HTML version fetched); marked UNKNOWN where relevant

papers.title: Learning to Evict from Key-Value Cache
papers.code_source: https://github.com/apple/ml-learning-to-evict (URL from abstract page; repository not inspected)
papers.offline_supervision: 'Trained on pre-computed generation traces' with 'a holistic reward, derived from future utility, that evaluates the quality of the ranking across all cache budgets'; lightweight per-head RL agents.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH -- 'learn future token utility offline, deploy a lightweight predictor online' is the KVP backbone M1 would sit on; a residual-over-baseline reformulation of this is architecturally cosmetic (the baseline score can simply be a feature)
papers.m2_overlap: UNKNOWN -- whether rankings are cache-state-conditional not determinable from the abstract
papers.m3_overlap: LOW -- budget-agnostic reward is an allocation-adjacent idea
papers.strongest_overlap: M1's learned deployable future-utility predictor.
papers.substantive_distinction: Reward is observational future utility from traces (not controlled KV ablation); domain is long-context benchmarks (RULER, LongBench), not reasoning models; absolute ranking, no base-compressor correction.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'learn future token utility' for KV eviction with lightweight deployable policies
claims_surviving: M1 counterfactual-ablation supervision (checked: abstract describes trace-derived reward, no ablation; UNCERTAIN at full-text level)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2602.10238 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for reward, inputs, offline training, deployment claims.
uncertainties: Full text and official code not inspected; cache-state conditionality and protected-reserve fields left null

papers.title: Locret: Enhancing Eviction in Long-Context LLM Inference with Trained Retaining Heads on Consumer-Grade Devices
authors: UNKNOWN -- author list not extracted from primary source this session
papers.offline_supervision: Retaining heads fine-tuned on frozen backbone with standard long-context SFT data to estimate a 'causal importance score (CIS)'; <1 GPU-hour offline cost.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- 'learned heads estimating causal importance for eviction' overlaps M1's learned-protection vocabulary, though its 'causal importance' label is not (per available evidence) derived from controlled KV ablation
papers.strongest_overlap: Learned deployable importance estimation for eviction.
papers.substantive_distinction: SFT-derived supervision, long-context domain, absolute scoring.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'learned retaining heads / learned causal KV importance' as a category
evidence: WebSearch results (2026-07-19) for 'Locret learned retaining heads KV cache eviction training causal importance' including arxiv.org/abs/2410.01805 abstract text.
uncertainties: Primary source not fetched; how Locret operationalizes 'causal importance' unverified -- marked UNKNOWN accordingly

papers.title: Adaptive Filtering of the KV Cache: Diagnosing and Correcting Structural-Role Bias in LLM Inference
authors: UNKNOWN -- author names not extracted from fetched HTML
intervention_type: counterfactual_role_suppression: ban all tokens of a role from retention and measure exact-match accuracy delta
cache_action: attention_masking_of_evicted_keys (no physical compaction in test harness)
papers.offline_supervision: Counterfactual role-ban experiments determine the deployed role budget shares; a 3.85M-parameter logistic probe classifies roles.
papers.m1_overlap: HIGH (conceptual) -- uses a controlled counterfactual suppression experiment to diagnose an existing deployable score's SYSTEMATIC misranking, then deploys a corrective layer on top of the base score; this is M1's diagnostic-to-correction pipeline, aimed at false positives rather than false negatives
papers.m3_overlap: LOW -- role-bucket budget shares are a constrained allocation over known signals
papers.strongest_overlap: M1's core conceptual move (intervention-diagnosed systematic error of a deployed score, corrected by an added layer at matched budget) already exists in print.
papers.substantive_distinction: Domain is schema-dense structured-data QA, not reasoning traces; corrections target over-retained structural noise (false positives), not causally-load-bearing evicted states (false negatives); role classes are static lexical categories, not causal-utility predictions; masking-only harness, no realized memory saving.
claims_killed: M1 sub-claim: novelty of 'controlled suppression experiment identifies systematic errors of an existing KV importance score, and a deployed corrective layer fixes them'
claims_surviving: M1 false-negative direction with per-state causal-utility labels (checked: this paper's counterfactual is role-level ban, labels are role classes, direction is false positives)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2607.13205 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes on role-ban counterfactual, role-conditional allocator, probe, 'the probe is not a drop-in replacement'.
uncertainties: Author list not captured; fetch-summarizer mediated

papers.title: CAOTE: KV Cache Selection for LLMs via Attention Output Error-Based Token Eviction
papers.offline_supervision: None -- training-free, closed-form.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- 'act as a meta-heuristic method with flexible usage with any token eviction method' already occupies the correct-an-existing-eviction-score-at-deployment slot, via an analytic estimate of the very quantity (output error caused by eviction) M1 would estimate empirically
papers.m2_overlap: MEDIUM -- the attention-output error of removing a token is a function of the currently retained set, so scores are implicitly cache-state-conditional
papers.strongest_overlap: Correcting an existing eviction score with information about the consequence of eviction, deployed on top of arbitrary base methods.
papers.substantive_distinction: Analytic one-step attention-output error, not a learned predictor of measured downstream behavioral utility; no offline counterfactual labels; no rescue partition.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'combine value information with attention scores to correct eviction decisions' (CAOTE does it in closed form)
claims_surviving: M1 learned residual from measured downstream-behavior ablation labels (checked: CAOTE is training-free and one-step-analytic)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2504.14051 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes on meta-heuristic usage, closed-form value integration.
uncertainties: Full text not inspected; per-step refresh mechanics inferred from the definition of the score, marked accordingly

papers.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
papers.code_source: https://github.com/terarachang/VaSE (URL from abstract page; repository not inspected)
intervention_type: empirical_eviction_failure_observation (evicting large-magnitude values causes repetitive loops)
papers.offline_supervision: None -- training-free.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- already implements 'protect a small set of states whose eviction causes catastrophic reasoning failure' on reasoning models at matched memory (outperforming R-KV), using a cheap signal instead of a learned residual
papers.strongest_overlap: Protected partition for eviction-catastrophic states in reasoning models; FlashAttention2-compatible; static footprint.
papers.substantive_distinction: Signal is value magnitude + stochasticity, not learned causal-utility residual; failure evidence is aggregate, not per-state counterfactual labels.
claims_killed: Generic claims: 'use value magnitude', 'add stochastic diversity', 'protect eviction-catastrophic reasoning states' as signals
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2606.03928 (2026-07-19); A3 matrix record vase_2606_03928 (prior verification).
uncertainties: Full text not inspected this session

papers.title: Information-Aware KV Cache Compression for Long Reasoning
papers.offline_supervision: None found -- 'No trainable predictor components are mentioned.'
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- 'Forward Influence... how compressed tokens affect future contexts' occupies the future-effect-of-compression signal space with a cheap observational proxy, on reasoning models (DeepSeek-R1)
papers.strongest_overlap: Future-influence-aware scoring for long reasoning without any learned component.
papers.substantive_distinction: Observational information-theoretic proxies, no counterfactual labels, no correction of a base compressor.
claims_killed: Generic claims: 'use entropy', 'use forward influence' as importance signals for reasoning KV compression
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2606.26875 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for Forward Influence and entropy integration.
uncertainties: Full text not inspected; online/offline timing details unresolved

papers.title: Epiphany-Aware KV Cache Eviction Without the Attention Matrix
papers.offline_supervision: None -- 'requires no training, classifier, or custom kernel'.
papers.m1_overlap: LOW -- occupies the hidden-state-change signal family on reasoning benchmarks
papers.strongest_overlap: Cheap deployable reasoning-shift signal without attention, FlashAttention-compatible.
papers.substantive_distinction: Observational signal; no supervision, no correction, no partition (as far as the abstract shows).
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'use hidden-state change' as a reasoning-aware importance signal
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2606.26472 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for epiphany score, FlashAttention compatibility, no-training statement.
uncertainties: Cache action details and protection behavior not in abstract; marked null

papers.title: Protection Is (Nearly) All You Need: Structural Protection Dominates Scoring in Globally Capped KV Eviction
papers.code_source: https://github.com/gpgabriel25/KVCacheBoundaryProtection (URL from abstract page; repository not inspected)
papers.offline_supervision: None.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM (as an effect-size threat, not an operation overlap) -- if 'scoring differences are secondary once boundaries are guarded' and score-isolation variants are TOST-equivalent to LRU under protection, the marginal value of any refined scoring correction (M1's whole payoff) may be empirically small
papers.m3_overlap: LOW -- fixed reserved-share allocation is a trivial-but-published allocation scheme
papers.strongest_overlap: Budgeted protected reserve inside a globally capped eviction budget; direct evidence that scoring refinements can be second-order.
papers.substantive_distinction: Protection is positional/structural, not causal-utility-based; domain is LongBench, not reasoning traces.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'reserve a budgeted protected partition inside a capped KV budget' as a novel cache operation
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2605.18053 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for boundary reserve, collapse without protection, TOST equivalence.
uncertainties: Whether the second-order-scoring finding transfers to reasoning-trace decode-time eviction is untested

papers.title: Do LLMs Encode Functional Importance of Reasoning Tokens?
papers.code_source: UNKNOWN -- none found on abstract page
cache_action: none -- diagnostic + distillation-data construction, no KV-cache policy deployed
papers.offline_supervision: Greedy pruning ranks; attention shown to predict them.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- establishes that iteratively recomputed functional importance of reasoning tokens exists, is measurable by likelihood objectives, and is predictable from cheap signals (attention); the 'importance conditional on what else has been removed' idea is inherent in greedy iterative deletion
papers.m2_overlap: MEDIUM -- greedy iterative removal IS deletion-conditional importance, at text level
papers.strongest_overlap: Deletion-conditional reasoning-token importance measurement (text-level).
papers.substantive_distinction: Removes visible tokens, not KV states; no deployed compression policy; no base-compressor false-negative framing.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'iteratively recompute reasoning-token importance conditional on previous deletions' as a measurement idea
claims_surviving: KV-state (as opposed to visible-token) deletion-conditional scoring inside a deployed policy (checked: no KV policy here) -- but that slot is filled by ForesightKV/NGC
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2601.03066 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for greedy pruning and attention-predicts-ranks.
uncertainties: Official code searched for on the abstract page only; not located

papers.title: Thought Anchors: Which LLM Reasoning Steps Matter?
cache_action: none -- interpretability method, no compression policy
papers.offline_supervision: None deployed.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- instance-specific counterfactual importance of reasoning steps (planning/uncertainty-management sentences as high-importance anchors) is established methodology M1's labels would replicate at KV level
papers.strongest_overlap: Counterfactual per-step reasoning importance measurement, instance-specific.
papers.substantive_distinction: Text-resampling intervention, no KV axis, no policy.
claims_killed: Generic claim: 'use counterfactual thought importance' as a measurement contribution
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2506.19143 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes for resampling method and thought-anchor finding.
uncertainties: Three-method enumeration (resampling / attention aggregation / attention suppression) known from the paper's structure but only resampling was quoted in the fetched abstract; not fully re-verified this session

papers.title: R-KV: Redundancy-aware KV Cache Compression for Reasoning Models
papers.version_reviewed: as pinned in third_party/R-KV submodule commit 45eaa7d69d20b7388321f077020a610d9afb65bd; NeurIPS 2025 per official repo
papers.offline_supervision: None -- training-free.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- as the intended BASE policy b_i, not a competitor; note its scores are already recomputed at each compaction event over the currently retained cache (docs/UPSTREAM_AUDIT.md)
papers.m2_overlap: HIGH -- per-compaction rescoring over the current retained cache means 'importance is refreshed under the compressed context' is already true of the base method M2 would claim to improve
papers.strongest_overlap: M2: the base compressor already rescores each compaction on the current cache state.
papers.substantive_distinction: No learned component, no counterfactual supervision, no explicit interaction-effect modeling between eviction decisions.
claims_killed: M2 sub-claim: novelty of refreshing importance scores under the current compressed cache at each compaction (already standard practice in the base method itself, and in H2O-lineage iterative eviction generally)
evidence: Local audit of pinned submodule (docs/UPSTREAM_AUDIT.md H1-H8, file:line citations)
A3 matrix record rkv_2505_24133.

papers.title: LazyEviction: Lagged KV Eviction with Attention Pattern Observation for Efficient Long Reasoning
authors: author list not extracted (A3 carried forward)
importance_signal: token_importance_recurrence_observation (tokens regain high attention after a delay)
papers.offline_supervision: None.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- 'tokens that look unimportant now become important later' is exactly the temporal half of the causal-false-negative failure mode, already named (Token Importance Recurrence) and mitigated with a deployable mechanism
papers.m2_overlap: MEDIUM -- lagged eviction is a dynamic, time-conditional importance treatment
papers.strongest_overlap: Deployable mitigation of delayed-importance false negatives in reasoning traces.
papers.substantive_distinction: Observational recurrence statistics, not counterfactual utility; no learned correction of a base score.
claims_killed: M1 sub-claim: novelty of the 'delayed-importance false negative' failure-mode observation in reasoning KV eviction
evidence: A3 matrix record lazyeviction_2506_15969 (verified 2026-07-19 in Phase A3); resurfaced in this session's B0 searches.

papers.title: Neural Garbage Collection: Learning to Forget while Learning to Reason
intervention_type: policy_gradient_on_task_reward; replay attention masks reported in search snippet as parallel eviction-visibility evaluation (unconfirmed in fetch)
papers.offline_supervision: Policy-gradient training against task reward.
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- end-to-end learned eviction for reasoning under task reward
papers.m2_overlap: HIGH -- cache-state-conditional learned eviction decisions during reasoning ('conditioned on the current cache state ... rather than using static heuristics')
papers.strongest_overlap: M2: conditional, learned, reasoning-domain eviction.
papers.substantive_distinction: Task-reward RL, not counterfactual per-state labels; no base-compressor correction.
claims_killed: M2 supporting kill: cache-state-conditional learned eviction for reasoning models exists (with ForesightKV, independently)
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/pdf/2604.18002 (2026-07-19): summary-level extraction with cache-state-conditionality and reasoning-model targeting confirmed; replay-mask detail left unconfirmed.
uncertainties: Replay-attention-mask mechanism not verbatim-confirmed; fetch was summary-level over PDF

papers.title: IndexMem: Learned KV-Cache Eviction with Latent Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
papers.offline_supervision: UNKNOWN
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM -- 'compensate for the attention contributions lost through KV eviction' is a repair-the-eviction-damage mechanism adjacent to false-negative rescue
papers.strongest_overlap: Eviction-loss compensation.
papers.substantive_distinction: UNKNOWN at snippet level.
evidence: WebSearch snippet (2026-07-19): 'lightweight latent memory module that compresses evicted tokens into a compact, online-updated state and provides residual readouts to compensate for the attention contributions lost through KV eviction.'
uncertainties: Screened only; would require full review before any M1-adjacent claim is made in a future phase

papers.title: Quantized Reasoning Models Think They Need to Think Longer, but They Do Not
papers.compression_family: quantization -- NOT KV eviction; weight-only PTQ (GPTQ, AWQ, 3-/4-bit) and end-to-end weight+activation+KV quantization (FlatQuant, W4A4KV4/W8A8KV8)
cache_action: none -- fix is a logit penalty on output distribution, not a cache operation; FlatQuant quantizes KV values but performs no per-state rescue or eviction
papers.offline_supervision: N/A -- diagnostic paper, not a trained cache policy
papers.m1_overlap: MEDIUM (diagnostic only, not method) -- position-level KL/entropy/logit-margin under a same-prefix matched-generation control is close kin to a controlled-intervention causal-utility measurement, but this paper measures quantization noise, not per-state KV-ablation utility, and proposes no cache operation
papers.strongest_overlap: Not a method threat (no cache action). The threat is a measurement-validity confound: 'in up to 52% of the quantized models' failures, models reach the right answer in intermediate reasoning steps but do not output it as a final answer,' and 'position-level KL divergence correlates strongly with the BF16 model's next-token entropy (Spearman rho=0.92)' -- i.e., compression-induced behavior change can be explained by entropy-amplified branching at locally uncertain tokens, independent of any per-state information loss.
papers.substantive_distinction: Different compression family entirely (quantization, not KV eviction); no cache operation of any kind; cannot be credited or debited against M1/M2/M3 novelty. Its role is as a required control for any future causal-false-negative claim (docs/METHOD_PIVOT_SPEC.md 5a, docs/B0_5_DISCOVERY_PROTOCOL.md baseline signals).
evidence: WebFetch of arxiv.org/abs/2606.00206 (2026-07-19): abstract quotes on the 52% intermediate-answer-abandoned failure mode and the training-free logit-penalty fix.

WebFetch of arxiv.org/html/2606.00206 (2026-07-19): verbatim quotes on evaluated models (DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen 1.5B/7B/14B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama 8B, QwQ-32B), quantization methods (GPTQ/AWQ weight-only vs FlatQuant W4A4KV4/W8A8KV8 end-to-end), same-prefix matched-generation control, and the entropy/KL/logit-margin diagnostic.
uncertainties: Fetch-summarizer mediated, per this file's schema_notice; full appendix and exact benchmark list not independently read
